# Prompt 12 - Business Intelligence & Analytics Layer

# Enterprise Space Mission Data & AI Platform

> **Phase 12 - Business Intelligence & Analytics**

---

# Objective

You are acting as:

- Principal BI Architect
- Analytics Engineering Lead
- Data Visualization Expert
- Product Analytics Architect

Your task is to design the complete Business Intelligence layer for the Space Mission Data & AI Platform.

The BI layer must transform trusted Gold datasets into executive dashboards, operational monitoring, engineering analytics, and decision-support reports.

This is an IMPLEMENTATION phase: build Superset assets-as-code plus supporting docs.

---

# Critical Rules

- This is an IMPLEMENTATION phase: build Superset datasets, charts, dashboards (assets-as-code)
- Write certified dataset SQL; export dashboards as YAML/JSON for version control
- Align with previous phases; Apache Superset; Docker Desktop (16 GB RAM)

---

# Context

Available data includes:

- Gold Layer datasets
- Trusted KPIs
- Semantic layer
- Data Products
- Time-series telemetry
- Mission analytics
- Space weather analytics
- Earth observation metadata

These datasets are now ready for business consumption.

---

# Task 1 - BI Strategy

Define:

- BI vision
- Self-service analytics
- Enterprise reporting strategy
- KPI governance
- Dashboard lifecycle

Explain why BI is critical for space operations.

---

# Task 2 - Dashboard Catalog

Design dashboards for:

## Executive Dashboard

Include:

- Mission Success Rate
- Satellite Availability
- Platform Health
- SLA Compliance
- Active Missions

---

## Satellite Operations Dashboard

Include:

- Telemetry Status
- Satellite Health
- Orbit Stability
- Sensor Performance
- Communication Availability

---

## Launch Operations Dashboard

Include:

- Launch Success Rate
- Launch Timeline
- Delays
- Failure Causes
- Vehicle Statistics

---

## Space Weather Dashboard

Include:

- Solar Activity
- Radiation Index
- Geomagnetic Storms
- Weather Alerts

---

## Earth Observation Dashboard

Include:

- Image Availability
- Coverage
- Observation Frequency
- Geographic Distribution

---

## Platform Operations Dashboard

Include:

- Pipeline Success
- Data Freshness
- Processing Latency
- Failed Jobs
- Resource Usage

---

# Task 3 - KPI Catalog

For every KPI define:

- Business definition
- Formula
- Source tables
- Refresh frequency
- Owner
- Thresholds
- Business impact

---

# Task 4 - Dashboard Design Standards

Define standards for:

- Layout
- Navigation
- Filters
- Drill-down
- Cross-filtering
- Accessibility
- Color conventions
- Time filtering

---

# Task 5 - User Personas

Define dashboard experiences for:

- Executives
- Mission Operators
- Data Engineers
- Data Scientists
- Platform Engineers
- Analysts

Explain access requirements for each.

---

# Task 6 - Dataset Strategy

Define:

- Certified datasets
- Shared datasets
- Personal datasets
- Virtual datasets
- Materialized datasets

Explain lifecycle and ownership.

---

# Task 7 - Reporting Strategy

Design:

- Daily reports
- Weekly reports
- Monthly reports
- Real-time monitoring
- Scheduled exports

---

# Task 8 - Alerting Strategy

Define alerts for:

- Pipeline failures
- Satellite anomalies
- Weather events
- Mission failures
- SLA breaches
- Resource utilization

Explain notification flow.

---

# Task 9 - Performance Optimization

Define:

- Dashboard caching
- Query optimization
- Materialized views
- Refresh scheduling
- Dataset optimization

---

# Task 10 - BI Security

Define:

- RBAC
- Row-level security
- Dataset permissions
- Dashboard permissions
- Audit logging

---

# Task 11 - Production Incident Scenarios

Create at least 10 incidents.

Examples:

- Dashboard loading slowly
- Missing KPIs
- Incorrect metrics
- Stale dashboards
- Broken filters
- Failed scheduled reports
- Dataset unavailable
- Unauthorized access
- Cache corruption
- Visualization rendering errors

Include:

- Symptoms
- Root cause
- Resolution
- Prevention

---

# Task 12 - Trade-off Analysis

Discuss:

- Live dashboards vs cached dashboards
- Materialized views vs direct queries
- Self-service BI vs governed BI
- Operational dashboards vs executive dashboards

---

# Task 13 - Architecture Decision Records

Create at least five ADRs.

Examples:

- Why Apache Superset
- Why semantic layer
- Why certified datasets
- Why dashboard standards
- Why governed KPIs

---

Generate:

bi/

datasets/
├── sql/                    certified dataset SQL
├── certified-datasets.md
├── dataset-governance.md

superset/
├── dashboards/             exported dashboard YAML/JSON
├── charts/

├── satellite-dashboard.md
├── launch-dashboard.md
├── weather-dashboard.md
├── earth-observation-dashboard.md
├── platform-dashboard.md

---

# Documentation Standards

- Enterprise Markdown
- Mermaid dashboard hierarchy diagrams
- KPI catalog tables
- Dashboard wireframes (Mermaid or ASCII)
- SLA tables
- User-role matrices

---

# Acceptance Criteria

Complete only if:

- Dashboard catalog is complete
- KPI catalog is fully defined
- User personas are documented
- BI security is defined
- Alerting strategy exists
- Reporting strategy exists
- Production incidents are documented

---

# Definition of Done

This phase is complete when:

> All Superset dashboards, certified dataset SQL, and exports are built, version-controlled, and load against Gold data.

Stop after completing this phase. Do NOT proceed to AI/ML implementation.
